title: TC001-Sign in with valid credentials and reach the correct dashboard
description: Verifies an existing user can log in with a valid email and password and is routed to the appropriate dashboard for their role.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200")
        
        # -> Navigate directly to the login page at /login and wait for the SPA to load (http://localhost:4200/login).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to /dashboard after login to show the user's dashboard."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The login page could not be reached because the SPA did not render, so the login form is not available and the test cannot proceed.

Observations:
- Navigated to http://localhost:4200/login but there are 0 interactive elements on the page.
- Waited 5 seconds and the page remained blank (screenshot shows empty content).

title: TC003-Request password reset shows privacy-preserving confirmation
description: Submitting a forgot-password request shows a generic confirmation message regardless of whether the email exists.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200")
        
        # -> Navigate to /login to find the login page and the 'forgot password' link.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/login")
        
        # -> Reload the /login page to try to get the SPA to render, wait for the UI to settle, then check for interactive elements (looking for a 'Forgot password' link).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'If an account with that email exists, you will receive an email with instructions to reset your password.')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The page should display a privacy-preserving confirmation that reset instructions will be sent if the account exists after submitting the forgot-password request"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The feature could not be reached because the login page did not render.

title: TC005-Register recruiter using an invite code with auto-login
description: A recruiter can register using a valid invite code and is automatically logged in to the recruiter dashboard after submitting valid details.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200")
        
        # -> Navigate to the registration page (/register).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/register")
        
        # -> Try reloading the app to recover the SPA UI by navigating to the site root (http://localhost:4200).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200")
        
        # -> Navigate to /register and wait for the page to finish loading (final wait attempt). If the page remains empty, report the feature as inaccessible and mark the test blocked.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/register")
        
        # -> Try a different navigation path to reach the registration UI by clicking the 'Get Started' button in the header.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/div/app-navbar/header/nav/div[2]/a[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Company Sign Up' link to switch the form from Candidate to Company/Recruiter registration (interactive element index 480).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/div/main/app-register/main/div[3]/div[2]/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the company/recruiter registration page (navigate to /register/company) and wait for the company form to appear so I can inspect the visible fields (to find the invite-code input).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/register/company")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to the recruiter dashboard after submitting valid registration details"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The recruiter invite-code field is not present on the company/recruiter registration form, so I could not complete or verify registration via an invite code.

Observations:
- The company/recruiter registration page loaded and shows fields for first name, last name, email, password, company name, industry, website, tax number, description, and logo upload, but there is no invite-code input visible.
- I scrolled and searched the page for 'invite' but found no control or field to enter an invite code.
- The form's submit button ('Complete Registration') is present, but without any invite-code field there is no way to perform the invited-recruiter registration path.

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode

        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # -> Navigate directly to the registration page at /register so the registration form can be located.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/register")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state

        if pw:

        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # -> Navigate to /register and load the registration page so the form fields are visible.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/register")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Password is too weak')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The registration should show a password strength validation error after submitting a weak password."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

title: TC014-Discover product and start registration
description: Verifies a public visitor can view the landing page and navigate into registration to start onboarding.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200")
        
        # -> Navigate to /landing to load the landing page content and then re-evaluate UI elements.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/landing")
        
        # -> Try to let the SPA load and then reload the page if still blank. First wait a bit longer, then reload the root URL to attempt a full load.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200")
        
        # -> Wait briefly, then navigate to /landing to attempt to load the SPA and reveal interactive elements.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/landing")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Register')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The registration page should be displayed after clicking the call-to-action to register"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
